cases.title: GraphInterrupt Not Re-raised in awrap_tool_call Wrapper Path

case.observed_action: ToolNode's awrap_tool_call wrapper path catches GraphInterrupt in a generic except Exception block and passes it to handle_tool_errors, producing a ToolMessage with error content instead of suspending the graph.
case.expected_boundary: GraphInterrupt (a GraphBubbleUp subclass) must be re-raised untouched at any exception boundary inside tool execution, including inside awrap_tool_call middleware, so the graph suspends instead of treating it as a tool failure.
case.runtime_context: LangGraph 1.2.6, Python 3.12, a ToolNode configured with both awrap_tool_call middleware and handle_tool_errors, executing a tool that calls interrupt() to pause for human approval.
case.evidence: Removing awrap_tool_call restores correct suspension behavior, and the printed output literally shows the Interrupt payload stringified inside a 'Tool call failed (GraphInterrupt)' error message rather than the graph halting.

commercial_action.label: Request a private agent failure audit
commercial_action.routing_rule: Use the public artifact for broadly useful redacted failures; use the private audit path when the evidence is sensitive or the operator needs a concrete fix, guardrail, or receipt proof.

cases.next_proof_step: Convert the submitted failure into the smallest reusable control: a deterministic guardrail, receipt proof, public autopsy entry, or private diagnosis if evidence is sensitive.

public_safety.redaction: emails, obvious API tokens, and long opaque secrets are redacted before publish
public_safety.status: public-safe stub; deeper diagnosis still requires evidence review

rule: Every qualifying external production-agent failure should become a redacted case artifact or a private-audit handoff.
